title: Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act
plain_summary: This bill says that if a baby is born alive during an abortion, doctors and nurses must give the baby the same medical care they would give any other newborn baby. They must also take the baby to a hospital right away. If healthcare workers don't follow these rules, they could go to jail for up to five years. If someone kills a baby that was born alive, they can be charged with murder. The mother cannot be punished under this law, but she can sue if healthcare workers break these rules.
last_action_text: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Findings and Constitutional Authority
sections.text: Congress has made these findings. First, if an abortion results in a baby being born alive, that baby is a legal person under all United States laws and has the right to all legal protections. Second, any baby born alive after an abortion or in a hospital, clinic, or other medical place has the same right to legal protection as any other newborn baby or any person who comes to a hospital for care. Congress has the power to make this law under two parts of the Constitution. One is section five of the Fourteenth Amendment, which gives Congress the power to make sure the government treats all people equally under the law. The other is section eight of Article One, which gives Congress the power to make laws needed to carry out the powers the Constitution gives it, including the power to regulate business between states.

sections.title: Section 3 Born-Alive Infants Protection
sections.text: This section adds new rules to federal criminal law about babies born alive after abortions. If an abortion or attempted abortion results in a baby born alive, any healthcare worker present must do two things. First, they must use the same level of professional skill, care, and effort to keep the baby alive and healthy as they would for any other baby born at the same stage of development. Second, after providing this care, they must make sure the baby is immediately taken to and admitted to a hospital. Any healthcare worker or employee of a hospital, doctor's office, or abortion clinic who knows about a failure to follow these rules must immediately report it to state or federal law enforcement, or both. Anyone who violates these rules can be fined and imprisoned for up to five years. Anyone who intentionally kills or tries to kill a baby born alive will be punished the same as someone who commits murder. The mother of a baby born alive cannot be prosecuted under this law or for conspiracy to violate this law. If a baby is born alive and these rules are violated, the woman who had the abortion can sue the person who violated the rules. She can get money for all physical and psychological injuries caused by the violation, plus three times the cost of the abortion, plus punitive damages. If she wins the lawsuit, she gets her attorney fees paid. If she loses and the court finds her lawsuit was frivolous, she must pay the other side's attorney fees. The law defines abortion as using any instrument, medicine, drug, or device to intentionally kill an unborn child of a pregnant woman, or to intentionally end the pregnancy for reasons other than producing a live birth after the baby could survive outside the womb or removing a dead unborn child. An attempt means conduct that represents a major step toward performing an abortion. This section also updates the headings in the federal criminal code to change from covering only partial-birth abortions to covering abortions generally.
